title: Introduction to C#
content:
### What is C#?

C# (pronounced "C Sharp") is a modern, object-oriented programming language developed by Microsoft as part of the .NET ecosystem. It was released in 2000 and has evolved significantly over the years.

### Key Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Object-Oriented | Supports classes, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation |
| Type-Safe | Strongly typed language with compile-time type checking |
| Cross-Platform | Runs on Windows, Linux, macOS via .NET |
| Garbage Collected | Automatic memory management |
| Async/Await | Built-in asynchronous programming support |

### Why Learn C#?

C# is widely used for:

- **Desktop applications** with WPF and WinForms
- **Web applications** with ASP.NET
- **Game development** with Unity
- **Mobile apps** with Xamarin / .NET MAUI
- **Cloud services** with Azure
- **Microservices** and enterprise software

> **Key Point:** C# combines the power of C++ with the simplicity of Visual Basic, making it a versatile language for beginners and experts alike.

### C# Evolution Timeline

```
C# 1.0 (2002) - Basic OOP features
C# 2.0 (2005) - Generics, nullable types
C# 3.0 (2007) - LINQ, lambda expressions
C# 4.0 (2010) - Dynamic binding, named arguments
C# 5.0 (2012) - Async/await
C# 6.0 (2015) - String interpolation, null-conditional operators
C# 7.0 (2017) - Pattern matching, tuples
C# 8.0 (2019) - Nullable reference types, async streams
C# 9.0 (2020) - Records, top-level statements
C# 10 (2021) - Global usings, file-scoped namespaces
C# 11 (2022) - Raw string literals, required members
C# 12 (2023) - Primary constructors, collection expressions
C# 13 (2024) - Params collections, new locking primitives
C# 14 (2026) - Experimental features in preview
```
